title: Linear Functions - Quiz
description: Test your understanding of slope, y-intercept, slope-intercept form, point-slope form, parallel/perpendicular lines, and linear models

questions.question: What is the slope of the line passing through (1, 3) and (4, 12)?
What is the y-intercept of the equation 2x + 3y = 12?
questions.explanation: Set x = 0: 3y = 12, so y = 4. The y-intercept is 4, meaning the line crosses the y-axis at (0, 4). In slope-intercept form: y = -2x/3 + 4.

questions.question: The point-slope form of a linear equation is:
questions.explanation: Point-slope form is y - y₁ = m(x - x₁), where m is the slope and (x₁, y₁) is a known point on the line. This form is useful when you know the slope and one point.

questions.question: Line A has slope 4. What is the slope of a line perpendicular to Line A?
questions.explanation: Perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals of each other. If one line has slope m, the perpendicular line has slope -1/m. So -1/4 is the perpendicular slope to 4.

questions.question: A horizontal line has a slope of:
questions.explanation: A horizontal line has zero slope because there is no vertical change (rise = 0). The equation is y = c (a constant). A vertical line, on the other hand, has undefined slope.

questions.question: Two parallel lines must have:

options.text: The same slope and the same y-intercept
The same slope but different y-intercepts

questions.explanation: Parallel lines have the same slope but different y-intercepts. If they had the same slope AND the same y-intercept, they would be the same line (coincident), not just parallel.

questions.question: Write the equation of a line with slope 2 passing through (3, 7) in slope-intercept form.
questions.explanation: Using point-slope form: y - 7 = 2(x - 3). Distribute: y - 7 = 2x - 6. Add 7: y = 2x + 1.

questions.question: If a car rental costs $30/day plus a $50 flat fee, which equation models the total cost C for d days?
questions.explanation: The $30/day is the variable cost (rate of change / slope), and the $50 flat fee is the fixed cost (y-intercept). The linear model is C = 30d + 50.

questions.question: What is the slope of the line defined by the equation y = -5x + 8?
questions.explanation: In slope-intercept form y = mx + b, the coefficient of x is the slope. Here m = -5. The negative slope indicates the line goes downward from left to right.

questions.question: A line has a slope of 0 and passes through the point (2, 5). What is its equation?
questions.explanation: A line with slope 0 is horizontal. Using y = mx + b: y = 0(x) + b = b. Since it passes through (2, 5), b = 5. The equation is y = 5.
